Legal_Regulatory_Terms_NER.definition: Global regulatory framework for banks to strengthen regulation, supervision, and risk management.
Extra capital banks must hold above minimum requirements to absorb losses during stress.
Variable capital buffer to protect banking sector during periods of excess credit growth.
Ensures banks hold enough high-quality liquid assets to survive a 30-day stress scenario.
Promotes stable funding over a one-year horizon to reduce funding risk.
Measures of a bank’s financial strength. Tier 1 is core capital; Tier 2 includes subordinated instruments.
Highest quality capital, including common shares and retained earnings.
Adjustment to the fair value of derivatives to account for counterparty credit risk.
Non-risk-based measure to restrict excessive leverage in the banking system.
Assets weighted by risk to determine minimum capital requirements.
